info.title: Context7 Documentation API
info.description: Search programming libraries and retrieve up-to-date documentation and code examples. Use search_libraries first to resolve a library name to a Context7 library ID, then use query_documentation to fetch relevant docs.

get.summary: Resolve a library name to a Context7-compatible library ID
get.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this tool before 'query_documentation' to obtain a valid library ID, UNLESS the user explicitly provides one in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

Each result includes:
- Library ID (format: /org/project)
- Name and description
- Code Snippets: number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: High, Medium, Low, or Unknown
- Benchmark Score: quality indicator (100 is the highest)
- Versions: available versions (format: /org/project/version)

Selection criteria (in order of priority):
1. Name similarity to the query (exact matches first)
2. Description relevance to the user's intent
3. Documentation coverage (prefer higher Code Snippet counts)
4. Source reputation (prefer High or Medium)
5. Benchmark Score

If multiple good matches exist, pick the most relevant one and explain why. If no good matches exist, suggest query refinements.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: The question or task the user needs help with. Used to rank library results by relevance. Example: 'How to set up authentication in Express.js'. Do not include sensitive information.

parameters.name: libraryName
parameters.description: Library or package name to search for (e.g. 'react', 'fastapi', 'next.js', 'langchain').

200.description: Search results with matching libraries ranked by relevance.

id.description: Context7-compatible library ID (format: /org/project). Pass this to query_documentation.

codeSnippets.description: Number of available code examples. Higher is better.

sourceReputation.description: Authority indicator of the documentation source.

versions.description: Available versions (format: /org/project/version). Use if the user asks for a specific version.

get.summary: Retrieve documentation and code examples for a library
get.description:
Fetches up-to-date, reranked documentation and code examples for the specified library.

You MUST call 'search_libraries' first to obtain a valid Context7 library ID, UNLESS the user explicitly provides one in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

Be specific in your query for best results. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js'. Bad: 'auth'.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best information you have.

parameters.name: query
parameters.description: Specific question or topic to retrieve documentation for. Be detailed. Example: 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Do not include sensitive information.

parameters.name: libraryId
parameters.description: Context7-compatible library ID obtained from search_libraries (e.g. '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87').

200.description: Documentation content with code examples as plain text.
